leads.typical_suppliers: Procurement is centralized at Fox Restaurant Concepts HQ in Phoenix under SVP Marji Aron (development/design/construction/facilities all roll up to her). Designer-led prototype: Studio Murnane (Phoenix) handles brand, architecture, and interiors for North Italia (per studiomurnane.com/portfolio/north-italia). General contractor relationships historically include Jokake Construction and MCI Builders in Arizona (both have True Food Kitchen — sister FRC brand — case studies showing the FRC GC stable). The model is owner-rep / direct: FRC corporate buys FF&E centrally and ships to GC; this is NOT a PE-consolidated platform despite Cheesecake Factory ownership — FRC operates with autonomy out of Phoenix. Sources: https://studiomurnane.com/portfolio/north-italia ; https://jokake.com/projects/true-food-kitchen/ .
leads.refurb_profile: North Italia's design language is warm modern Italian: hand-finished hardwood tabletops, leather and upholstered banquettes, mixed-material bar fronts (wood + tile + metal), woven/cane chair backs, and statement custom lighting. Each location is layered with local art (e.g., Walnut Creek and Katy locations feature commissioned local artist work — confirmed in https://contracosta.news/2026/03/25/north-italias-first-bay-area-restaurant-now-open-in-walnut-creek/ and https://myneighborhoodnews.com/north-italia-katy-opening-date-announced). FF&E categories where Higney International fits cleanly: solid-hardwood tabletops, custom banquettes, dining and bar chairs/stools, decorative millwork (host stands, wine displays, bar back). Materials skew hardwood (not laminate) and real leather/upholstery (not vinyl) given the upscale-casual positioning. Cheesecake Factory parent has called out construction cost headwinds publicly — refurbishment of the 39-unit base could be a re-spec opportunity if cost-equivalent.
leads.expansion_intel: Confirmed-in-build / not-yet-open units beyond standard pipeline: (1) Katy TX at LaCenterra at Cinco Ranch — $1.87M renovation, ~9,000 sq ft / 300 seats, construction started Nov 2025, scheduled open June 10, 2026 (https://myneighborhoodnews.com/north-italia-katy-opening-date-announced-what-the-summer-2026-launch-means-for-lacenterra-and-west-houston-dining ; https://communityimpact.com/houston/katy-fulshear/dining/2026/05/05/north-italia-slated-to-open-4th-greater-houston-location-in-la-centerra/). (2) Huntsville AL at Bridge Street Town Centre — taking over former Bravo! Italian space, no firm open date (https://www.rocketcitynow.com/article/news/local/north-italia-to-take-over-bridge-street-space-left-by-bravos-closure/525-c48cf51c-700b-49ac-8d19-4ff9a260ff82). (3) Walnut Creek CA opened March 2026 — first Bay Area location, signal of West-coast push (https://contracosta.news/2026/03/25/north-italias-first-bay-area-restaurant-now-open-in-walnut-creek/). Cheesecake Factory has publicly stated 20%/year unit growth target for North Italia — at 39 locations that's ~8 new builds/yr (https://www.nrn.com/fast-casual/the-cheesecake-factory-weighs-growth-for-both-north-italia-and-flower-child).
leads.research_gap: Could not verify specific FF&E vendors currently on the North Italia spec sheet — FRC does not publicly list vendors. Would need either (a) outreach via Marji Aron directly, or (b) outreach to Studio Murnane as designer-of-record to get spec-in at prototype level. Also no public Director of FF&E / Procurement role — that function sits inside Marji Aron's group at FRC.

leads.typical_suppliers: Family-owned (Fuller family), NOT PE-consolidated — procurement runs out of Vancouver corporate. Design is in-house under Jennifer Hoffbeck (VP Design); construction was historically VP Construction & Facilities Sumeet Mittal (who has since moved to Academy Sports + Outdoors — meaning that seat may be open or recently re-filled — verify before pitching). General contractor on US new builds is project-specific: R.C. Mathews Contractor built the Nashville Yards location (https://rcmathews.com/news/grand-opening-of-earls-kitchen-bar-at-nashville-yards/). Earls' design vocabulary is heavily custom — Nashville features custom light fixtures, custom seating, and a sweeping wood-arch bar; Miami Dadeland was published in rd+d magazine (https://rddmag.com/design/227-web-exclusive-new-earls-kitchen-bar-rocks-miami-s-dadeland-mall) and Hospitality Design (https://hospitalitydesign.com/projects/f-b/earls-kitchen-bar/). Channel is designer-led-in-house: Hoffbeck specs, then sources via approved vendor list — meaning Higney International needs to land on her short-list. 300 Main location is also a published case study (https://www.reminetwork.com/articles/300-main-earls-kitchen-bar/).
leads.refurb_profile: Earls is one of the most design-rich targets in the Maggiano's framework. Recurring elements across recent builds: marble- and wood-topped tables, upholstered AND leather chair mix, custom banquettes with jewel-toned fabrics, large architectural wood elements (Nashville's wood arches over bar), mosaic tile floors, deep paneling (Seaport will feature 'ivy-green paneling'). They do NOT use laminate or vinyl in core dining — they're a real-wood, real-leather operator. Higney's hardwood tabletop + banquette + chair mix maps almost exactly to their spec. Sources: https://www.pembroke.com/news/earls-kitchen-bar-to-open-new-location-at-bostons-commonwealth-pier-in-2027/ ; https://nashvilleguru.com/193756/a-look-inside-earls-nashville . Refurb cycle is unclear but their oldest US locations (Assembly Row 2014, Pru Center 2017) are now 9-12 years old and likely entering refresh territory.
leads.expansion_intel: Beyond Boston Seaport 2027 (already in your lead notes — confirmed at 157 Seaport Blvd in Seaport West, 9,800 sq ft / three-season patio, Pembroke is landlord, https://www.pembroke.com/news/earls-kitchen-bar-to-open-new-location-at-bostons-commonwealth-pier-in-2027/): Nashville Yards opened April 16, 2026 — 260 seats, just opened so unlikely to be in your radar yet (https://www.visitmusiccity.com/media/press-release/2026/earls-kitchen-bar-to-open-april-16-nashville-yards). Earls has publicly stated 'further expansion across North America' but specific filed leases beyond Boston Seaport were not surfacing in news. Sister brand 'Birdies Eats & Drinks' is a new banner being developed by Earls (https://www.biv.com/news/hospitality-marketing-tourism/earls-launch-new-restaurant-banner-birdies-eats-drinks-8270403) — that's a potential separate FF&E opportunity worth scoping. Total Earls footprint is 68 locations Canada+US.
leads.research_gap: Could not confirm the current VP of Construction & Facilities by name — the prior VP (Sumeet Mittal) appears to have moved to Academy Sports + Outdoors per ZoomInfo, but no public announcement of his replacement at Earls. Worth a direct LinkedIn search or call to Vancouver HQ before pitching. Also could not find specific named FF&E vendors on Earls' current spec sheet — designer-led in-house procurement means access goes through Jennifer Hoffbeck.

Outdoor furniture is a meaningful category (large patios at every location). Materials: hardwood over laminate, but with weathered/coastal finishes rather than the dark-stained polish of Earls. leads.typical_suppliers: True Food Kitchen is PE-backed by Centerbridge Partners (long-time investor, formerly owned P.F. Chang's), HumanCo, and Manna Tree, with celebrity capital from Oprah Winfrey, Howard Schultz, Lion Capital, and Dr. Andrew Weil (https://www.nrn.com/casual-dining/true-food-kitchen-gets-100m-investment-from-humanco-and-manna-tree). The $100M 2022 raise was specifically earmarked for the new smaller-format fast-casual concept AND growth of the flagship. General contractors on True Food buildouts include Jokake Construction (Phoenix, https://jokake.com/projects/true-food-kitchen/) and MCI Builders (https://mcibuildersaz.com/portfolio-item/true-food-kitchen/), both Phoenix-based — reflects FRC roots (Sam Fox was original co-founder with Dr. Weil). Procurement was historically directed by Director of Design and Development Mike Peralta (per RocketReach, though that role was 'COVID layoff'-noted, so likely vacated). The CDO seat under Ryan Slemons starting May 4, 2026 is now THE point of entry — he will own real estate + store development including FF&E procurement decisions. Channel is moving toward owner-rep/direct under the new CDO structure.
leads.refurb_profile: True Food's design language is wellness-bright: light woods, lots of natural fiber (rattan, woven), plants integrated into the space, lighter upholstery palette (cream/sage/terracotta), open kitchens visible from dining. They use real hardwood tabletops in most locations (not laminate) — fits Higney spec. Banquettes tend to be lower-profile and lighter-upholstered than at Earls. Chairs lean toward bentwood / cane / light-finish wood. The new fast-casual concept (in development) per Christine Barone quotes wants 'guardrails of the brand applied to something more convenient and agile from a development angle' (https://www.restaurantbusinessonline.com/financing/true-food-kitchen-plans-launch-new-fast-casual-brand-help-100m-investment) — that translates to lower buildout cost, which may push the fast-casual format toward more laminate/composite, but the full-service flagship remains hardwood-spec.
leads.expansion_intel: True Food Kitchen is at ~46 units (up from 42 at time of $100M raise). The new unnamed fast-casual format was supposed to launch in Phoenix; status as of mid-2026 unclear from public reporting — worth pressing for. Jacksonville Jax Daily Record confirmed a Midtown location (https://www.jaxdailyrecord.com/news/2018/may/04/true-food-kitchens-healthy-concept/) but that's old. Tampa Midtown opened recently per Fox 13. Specific 2026/2027 leases beyond what's announced were not surfacing — the Ryan Slemons hire on May 4, 2026 likely signals a development re-acceleration, but the pipeline he's building is not yet public. The Oprah celebrity-backed marketing tailwind plus Centerbridge cash gives them capital but they have been growing slowly post-COVID.
leads.research_gap: Could not surface a named current Director of Design or Director of Construction — Mike Peralta's COVID layoff appears to have left that seat unfilled, and Slemons (CDO) is brand-new (May 4 start). The whole development org under him may not yet be fully staffed, which is an OPPORTUNITY rather than a gap — Higney can offer to be a known-vendor early as he builds. New fast-casual concept format and timing not confirmed publicly beyond 2024 announcements. Centerbridge's operating partner on the deal also not surfaced.
research_gap_notes: 2026-05-21 correction: Ryan Slemons (previously listed here as incoming CDO) joined SWEETGREEN as CDO effective May 4, 2026 — NOT True Food Kitchen. Aggregator data error. TFK CDO seat status unknown; recommend direct outreach to Jonathan Perez (CAO/GC) to identify development lead.

leads.typical_suppliers: Highly in-house. Soho House operates Soho Home — own furniture/casegoods brand (oak bar cabinets, marble dining tables, tufted bed frames, custom upholstery). Sordo Madaleno (Mexico City) architect for Los Cabos. Sugarhouse Design & Architecture (NYC) credits Soho House millwork/FF&E procurement.
leads.refurb_profile: Custom hardwood tabletops, banquettes, dining chairs, bar tops, upholstered lounge, custom millwork (oak bar cabinets signature), restaurant casegoods, hotel guestroom casegoods, rooftop furniture. Cecconi's in each House = recurring FF&E demand. Warm hardwoods (oak, walnut), brass/antiqued brass, marble, velvet/linen, vintage + bespoke. Tokyo introduced parquet/tatami-inspired, Kyoto lacquer, washi — open to regional materials.
leads.expansion_intel: CORRECTIONS: Tokyo OPENED April 6 2026 (42 bedrooms, Aoyama). Flatiron NYC is 2027 (NOT 2026) — largest US House. Holloway LA is 2026 'update' (pickleball + art), not full reno. Farmhouse NY (Rhinebeck, 250 acres, 48 bedrooms) 2027. Desert House Palm Springs 2027 (largest pool 180ft). Ranch House Sonoma 2027 (50 cottages, Kenwood CA). 2027 lineup = 4 large-scale ground-up FF&E packages being specified NOW in 2026. CEO Andrew Carnie publicly prioritizes North American expansion.
leads.research_gap: Soho House does not publicly disclose a procurement or FF&E director. Procurement flows through in-house design + Soho Home. Best access route is Nolan or property-level project lead.

strategy_overview.core_thesis: Peter face-to-face is the closer. Strategy = manufacturing chokepoints that put Peter in the same room as named decision-makers, with the minimum travel time per qualified meeting. Cold email at this seniority converts at ~0%; physical samples + industry-peer credibility + a designer who already trusts you walking you in = the only thing that works.

creative_moves.name: HI Showroom Dinner
creative_moves.summary: Half-day in Fort Lauderdale: factory tour + dinner with Peter. Fly 8 designers (not VPs) on HI's dime. Aria, Studio Murnane, NELSON, Meyer Davis principals. ~$8K. Two will become repeat specifiers within 12 months.

creative_moves.name: Active-Build Drop-In
creative_moves.summary: Show up at confirmed build sites (North Italia Katy TX, bartaco One Loudoun). Ask for GC, hand them a card + sample. GC tells designer 'this guy showed up with real wood.' High signal, ~$0 cost.

creative_moves.name: Lookbook Hand-Delivery
creative_moves.summary: Hard-copy lookbook — Nikki Beach, Hyatt Place Boca, Joia, Boatyard. Foil-stamped cover. ~$40/unit × 50. Peter never goes to a meeting without one. Decision-makers keep good lookbooks; they don't keep PDFs.

creative_moves.name: Maggiano's Tear-Sheet
creative_moves.summary: One-page: photo + spec + Peter's operating cost data on the Maggiano's framework. Does more in 5 min than 30 slides.

creative_moves.name: Specified Vendor Quiet Goal
creative_moves.summary: Each trip's explicit objective is NOT to close a brand — it's to get one designer firm to add HI to their internal specified-vendor list. Slow asset that compounds for years.

creative_moves.name: Architect Referral Bonus
creative_moves.summary: 1-2% referral fee per specified-by relationship, structured as 'design integration support fee.' Two firms saying yes pays for itself in one project.

creative_moves.name: Sam Fox 5-Minute Move
creative_moves.summary: Get 5 min with Sam Fox at HD Expo. Don't pitch. Ask: 'What's one material on your current spec sheet you'd replace if you could?' Respond within 48 hrs with a sample of the alternative. Opens the whole FRC universe.

ninety_day_sequence: Weeks 1-2: Build lookbook (50 units). Build Maggiano's tear-sheet. Get Peter's existing-network list.
Weeks 3-4: Designer-first warm outreach. Email Aria, Studio Murnane, Meyer Davis, NELSON, Shepley Bulfinch.
Weeks 5-8: Trip A — Phoenix. Anchor on Marji Aron.
Weeks 9-12: Trip B — Chicago. Anchor on Aria Group.
Weeks 13+: Trip C — NYC/Boston. Built around whichever introductions came out of A + B.
